title: Bootstrap x0x-symphony repo with architecture and ADRs
description: Create the sibling repo skeleton (Cargo.toml, README, CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, justfile, LICENSE), write the M0 architecture document at docs/design/symphony.md, and commit four ADRs that lock the structural decisions: tracker abstraction, sharded ownership with TTL, no external tracker in v1.0, and x0x TaskList as the symphony backbone.
acceptance: Repo at ../x0x-symphony exists with skeleton files
docs/design/symphony.md is committed and authoritative
docs/adr/0001..0004 are all marked Accepted
issues/{README.md, schema.md, issues.jsonl} are committed
WORKFLOW.md exists for symphony's own dogfooding
validation: Cross-check ADR statuses against the README index

handoff.summary: M0 architecture frozen: sibling repo bootstrapped, four ADRs accepted, design doc and convergence note committed, symphony-side bootstrap tracker seeded.

validation.command: python3 -c "import json,pathlib\nfor l in pathlib.Path('issues/issues.jsonl').read_text().splitlines():\n  if l.strip(): json.loads(l)"

follow_up: Decide on a GitHub remote and push (out of scope for M0)
Sync the Obsidian vault note for the new project

title: Define Tracker / Runner / Workspace traits as a runnable crate
description: Add the first crate `crates/x0x-symphony-core` to the workspace. Implement the trait surfaces from ADR-0001: Tracker, Runner, Workspace, plus the supporting types (Issue, Claim, Handoff, ReleaseReason, RunnerCapabilities, SessionContext, SessionHandle, TurnOutcome, UsageReport, EventStream, PollContext). Traits only — no adapter implementations yet. Ship full rustdoc on every public item. Every test must pass and clippy must be clean with -D warnings.
acceptance: crates/x0x-symphony-core/ exists in the workspace
Tracker, Runner, Workspace traits compile with documented public surface
No adapter implementations are landed in this issue
Crate has at least one trait round-trip test using a stub impl
just fmt-check && just lint && just test all pass

handoff.summary: Added the x0x-symphony-core crate with documented Tracker, Runner, and Workspace traits plus the shared issue, claim, handoff, runner, workflow, and workspace domain types required by ADR-0001.
follow_up: Cargo.toml clippy pedantic lint priority was adjusted so workspace lints work under `-D warnings` once crates are present.
No adapter implementations were added; only test-local stub implementations exist for trait round-trip coverage.
No production unwrap/expect/panic/todo/unimplemented/println/eprintln/dbg patterns were found in the new crate.

title: Implement git_jsonl Tracker adapter
description: Add `crates/x0x-symphony-tracker-git-jsonl` implementing the Tracker trait against `issues/issues.jsonl`. Use git index lock for serialization on a single host; commit each state transition. Includes deterministic ID allocation, blocker checks, and JSONL schema validation on read. Throwaway adapter — explicit module-level doc comment notes M1–M2 lifespan and points at ADR-0003.
acceptance: Adapter implements Tracker against issues/issues.jsonl
Round-trip integration test creates, claims, heartbeats, and hands off an issue
Schema violations are reported with structured errors, never panics
Module-level doc records lifespan and supersession path

title: Implement shell Runner with codex and claude_code presets
description: Add `crates/x0x-symphony-runner-shell` implementing the Runner trait by spawning a child process with prompt on stdin, streaming stdout/stderr, capturing exit + duration + minimal usage. Add two thin preset configurations (codex, claude_code) that set command + args + env without bespoke logic. Per ADR-0001, the shell runner is the canonical contract; bespoke runner crates are deferred until a harness's structured event stream is worth parsing.
acceptance: Adapter implements Runner against arbitrary child processes
codex and claude_code preset configs are documented and tested
Output streaming does not buffer stdout/stderr indefinitely
Hook timeout is enforced via tokio::time::timeout, not signals

title: Workspace manager with hook execution and root containment
description: Add `crates/x0x-symphony-workspace` with deterministic per-issue paths under a configured root, hook execution (after_create, before_run, after_run, before_remove) with timeouts, and root containment enforcement (sanitized issue IDs, refusal to operate on paths that escape the root). Workspaces are preserved across retries and cleaned on terminal states.
acceptance: Workspace paths are deterministic from sanitized issue IDs
Symbolic links and traversal sequences are rejected at creation
Hook timeouts are enforced and produce structured outcomes
Cleanup respects retry semantics — only terminal states delete

title: Orchestrator: poll loop, dispatch, concurrency, retry
description: Add `crates/x0x-symphony-orchestrator` wiring the Tracker + Runner + Workspace + claim manager. Single-node operation. Polling loop driven by polling.interval_ms. Concurrency limits per agent.max_concurrent_agents and per-state caps. Exponential backoff capped at agent.max_retry_backoff_ms. On startup, reconcile any in-progress claim owned by this agent (resume if heartbeat fresh, release if stale).
acceptance: End-to-end smoke test: pick a todo issue, run a stub shell runner, write review back
Concurrency caps are tested with a forced contention scenario
Reconciliation logic is tested with mocked time
No unwrap/expect/panic in production paths

title: Daemon and CLI binaries (x0x-symphonyd, x0x-symphony)
description: Add `crates/x0x-symphony-bin` exposing the daemon (`x0x-symphonyd`) and CLI (`x0x-symphony`). Daemon runs the orchestrator. CLI subcommands at minimum: tasks (list), claim, handoff, status, proofs (list/show), config (show/check). Both binaries read WORKFLOW.md for configuration; CLI talks to daemon over local HTTP.
acceptance: x0x-symphonyd starts, loads WORKFLOW.md, runs the poll loop
x0x-symphony tasks lists active issues
x0x-symphony status shows active claims and last events
Help text is generated and reviewed for every subcommand

title: M1 operator and runner-authoring guides
description: Write docs/symphony/operator.md (configuring WORKFLOW.md, starting the daemon, common operations) and docs/symphony/runner-authoring.md (the Runner trait, the shell runner contract, how to add a preset). Both must reference the design doc and ADRs rather than restating them. Update docs/symphony/README.md as an index.
acceptance: docs/symphony/operator.md exists and is consistent with shipped behaviour
docs/symphony/runner-authoring.md exists with a worked example
docs/symphony/README.md indexes both
Obsidian vault is synced
validation: Manual review against the running daemon

title: CI workflow: fmt, clippy, nextest, doc, audit
description: Add .github/workflows/ci.yml that runs fmt-check, clippy with -D warnings, nextest, doc with -D rustdoc warnings, and cargo-audit on push and PR. Mirror x0x's CI shape but without ant-quic / saorsa-gossip symlinks (this repo has no Cargo dependency on either). Use Rust 1.95.0 toolchain.
acceptance: ci.yml runs on push and PR to main
All five checks gate merges: fmt, clippy, nextest, doc, doc-test
CI runs `just check` AND `just doc-test` so the rustdoc-example invariant matches the local invariant
Toolchain pinned to 1.95.0
Workflow visibly green on a trivial PR before being marked complete

handoff.summary: Added the CI workflow for x0x-symphony. CI runs on pushes and pull requests to main, pins Rust 1.95.0, runs the canonical `just check`, runs `just doc-test` for rustdoc examples, and runs `cargo audit`.
follow_up: User explicitly requested direct pushes to main and no PRs, so the PR-run acceptance check is recorded as skipped; the workflow will validate on the direct main push instead.
CI uses `just check` as the aggregate fmt/clippy/nextest/doc gate and `just doc-test` as the rustdoc-example gate, matching the tightened XSY-0009 acceptance.

title: Justfile recipes filled in once first crate exists
description: Once XSY-0002 lands the first crate, replace the stub justfile recipes so check / fmt / lint / test / doc / build all execute against the workspace. Keep recipe names stable; operators rely on `just check` as the canonical validation entry point.
acceptance: just check runs all four checks against the real workspace
Recipe names match those documented in CLAUDE.md
No raw cargo commands needed for day-to-day development

handoff.summary: Replaced the bootstrap/stub justfile wording with real workspace validation recipes. All day-to-day recipes now target the Cargo workspace explicitly, deny warnings for build/test/lint paths, and keep `just check` as the canonical fmt + clippy + nextest + rustdoc entry point.
follow_up: `just doc-test` is available as an explicit extra recipe for rustdoc examples; it is not part of `just check` because the documented canonical check is fmt + lint + nextest + rustdoc build.
Updated CLAUDE.md to remove stale language saying the justfile recipes are stubs.

title: release.yml stub for multi-platform binaries
description: Add .github/workflows/release.yml that builds binaries for the standard target matrix (mirrors x0x's matrix) on tag push. M1 ships the stub only — actual publish jobs land at M5. Cross-compile via cargo-zigbuild for Linux targets.
acceptance: release.yml exists with the correct trigger (tag push)
Build matrix matches x0x's
Workflow is dry-runnable on a non-publishing test tag
No publish jobs wired yet (M5 work)
validation: Manual workflow dispatch on a test tag

title: security.yml: daily cargo-audit on main
description: Add .github/workflows/security.yml that runs cargo-audit on a daily schedule against main and on PR. Failures must surface in the PR check status. Mirrors x0x's security.yml.
acceptance: security.yml runs daily and on PR
Failures are visible in PR checks
Schedule trigger uses cron at 06:00 UTC
validation: Workflow run on schedule and PR

title: Shard assignment at issue creation
description: Implement `x0x-symphony issue new` that computes shard.primary and shard.backups by XOR distance against hash(task_id) over the configured worker list. M2 uses a static workers: list in WORKFLOW.md as a placeholder; M4 replaces this with live presence-based discovery. shard.created_view_epoch is recorded for audit. Subsequent edits to shard.* are rejected with a structured error.
acceptance: Issue creation populates shard.primary and shard.backups deterministically
XOR-distance computation has property tests covering uniformity
Shard fields are immutable after creation; edits return a structured error
Static workers: list documented loudly as a placeholder for M4

title: Heartbeat writer + TTL takeover with mocked-clock tests
description: Background task in the orchestrator refreshes claim.heartbeat_at every claim_ttl_ms / 4. Reconciliation on startup: if claim.by == self and heartbeat fresh, resume; if stale, release with reason expired_heartbeat; if owned by another agent, leave alone unless this agent is a backup AND TTL elapsed AND we want to claim. Backup-takeover branch must use the lower-index-wins tiebreak from ADR-0002. Mocked-clock test (tokio::time::pause) demonstrates each branch.
acceptance: Heartbeat refresh interval is claim_ttl_ms / 4
Mocked-clock tests cover: fresh-resume, stale-self-release, backup-takeover
Lower-index tiebreak is exercised in a forced-conflict test
No production unwrap/expect/panic

title: Validation artefact sink: proofs/<issue>/<ts>/
description: Every dispatch writes proofs/<issue>/<utc-ts>/ containing manifest.json (runner kind, preset, command, args, env-allowlist, exit code, duration, agent_id, hostname), stdout.log, stderr.log, plus any files emitted via RunnerArtifact events. manifest.json is machine-readable. Reaper not in scope (lands at M5).
acceptance: Every dispatch produces a complete proofs subtree
manifest.json schema is documented in docs/symphony/operator.md
RunnerArtifact events are persisted under the proofs dir
Reaper explicitly out of scope; comment marker references M5

title: Handoff writer with proofs_dir link
description: On successful runner completion, the orchestrator constructs a Handoff with summary, files_changed (from `git diff --name-only` inside workspace), validation (exit codes of configured validation commands), follow_up (from runner structured output if any), and proofs_dir (relative path). Tracker writes the handoff and moves state to review. ML-DSA-65 signing deferred to M3.
acceptance: Handoff includes summary, files_changed, validation, follow_up, proofs_dir
files_changed populated from git diff inside workspace
State transitions to review atomically with handoff write
Signing explicitly deferred with TODO referencing XSY-0020

title: Schema freeze: shard / claim / handoff fields documented and validated
description: Issue Rust struct mirrors issues/schema.md exactly. JSONL adapter validates on read and write; unknown fields are preserved verbatim. Property test asserts round-trip stability across the full Issue surface including shard, claim, handoff. ADR-0001 referenced; any new field after this needs a superseding ADR.
acceptance: Issue struct matches issues/schema.md field-by-field
Property test asserts JSON round-trip stability over an arbitrary-Issue strategy
Unknown fields are preserved verbatim across write cycles
schema.md updated to mark M2 as the freeze point
Decide the fate of ShardRole::ManualM1 introduced by XSY-0002 — either bless it as a permanent variant for hand-managed claims or remove it once Shard slates are real

title: Reconcile abandon-records on startup
description: Implement partition reunion logic from ADR-0002 even though M2 is single-host: when the orchestrator finds two valid claim records on the same issue, the lower-index slot wins; the loser's claim transitions to an abandon record (a sibling LWW field) citing the conflict. Unit-tested with synthetic dual-claim records.
acceptance: Lower-index slot wins on synthetic dual-claim
Loser writes a structured abandon record
Abandon record is rendered in proofs as <ts>-abandoned/
No production unwrap/expect/panic

title: Implement x0x_crdt Tracker adapter against x0xd REST/WS
description: New crate `crates/x0x-symphony-tracker-x0x-crdt`. Talks to x0xd over HTTP. fetch_candidates reads /task-lists/:id/tasks and decodes each TaskItem (checkbox + LWW metadata) into Issue. claim/heartbeat write metadata via PATCH /task-lists/:id/tasks/:tid. handoff writes metadata + optionally a KvStore blob via POST /stores/:id/:key. Subscribes to /events WebSocket for live updates.
acceptance: Adapter implements Tracker against a running x0xd
Round-trip integration test exercises create/claim/heartbeat/handoff
WebSocket subscription delivers live updates
Module-level doc points at ADR-0004

title: ML-DSA-65 signing + verification of claim and handoff payloads
description: Symphony reads the agent identity via GET /agent and signs claim and handoff payloads through a thin x0xd-side endpoint POST /agent/sign (which holds the agent key). Verification on read; mismatches drop the record with a WARN log. BLOCKED on x0x adding POST /agent/sign — track the corresponding x0x-side issue and link here when filed.
acceptance: Claims and handoffs are signed with the agent key via x0xd
Signature verification on read drops invalid records with WARN log
Cross-repo block on x0x:POST /agent/sign is documented and tracked

title: MLS-encrypted task-list dispatch (private project groups)
description: Per-project task lists may be MLS-encrypted. Symphony resolves WORKFLOW.md's tracker.group field to an MLS group, joins on first dispatch, and uses the group-scoped task list endpoints. Workers outside the group cannot see or claim. Integration test: two daemons in different MLS groups do not see each other's tasks.
acceptance: tracker.group resolves to an MLS group; symphony joins on first use
Workers outside the group return zero candidates from fetch_candidates
Two-daemon integration test covers group isolation
No symphony-side MLS key management — relies on x0xd primitives

title: Trust-gated dispatch using x0xd /agent + /contacts
description: Before dispatch, the orchestrator queries x0xd's GET /contacts/:agent_id and rejects claims from agents whose TrustLevel < Trusted for tasks labelled security-sensitive. Configurable per project in WORKFLOW.md (security.required_trust).
acceptance: Trust gate rejects claims from non-trusted agents on security-sensitive tasks
WORKFLOW.md security.required_trust is documented and respected
Integration test covers Blocked / Unknown / Known / Trusted / Pinned

title: x0x GUI board view: symphony filters and claim badges (PR to x0x)
description: Cross-repo deliverable. Open a PR against saorsa-labs/x0x extending renderSpaceBoard in src/gui/x0x-gui.html to: (a) read symphony metadata fields (shard, claim, state, priority, labels, handoff.proofs_dir) and render badges, (b) group cards into columns by state (todo / in_progress / review / done) instead of just by checkbox state, (c) expose a `symphony` filter toggle that hides non-symphony tasks. Branch name: symphony-board-view.
acceptance: PR opened on saorsa-labs/x0x with branch symphony-board-view
Board groups by state, not just by checkbox
Badges visible for shard role, claim freshness, validation status
Symphony filter toggle hides non-symphony tasks
PR merge coordinated with v0.0.M3 tag
validation: Manual GUI smoke against a running x0xd

title: Delete tracker-git-jsonl crate; tag v0.0.M3
description: Remove crates/x0x-symphony-tracker-git-jsonl/ from the workspace. Update workspace Cargo.toml and any references. Update operator guide to remove the JSONL section. Tag v0.0.M3. This is the M3 gate: the only tracker in the tree after this issue closes is x0x_crdt.
acceptance: No tracker/git_jsonl files remain in the tree
Workspace Cargo.toml updated
Operator guide removes JSONL section
Tag v0.0.M3 pushed

title: Worker advertisement on x0x/symphony/workers gossip topic
description: Each x0x-symphonyd publishes a signed WorkerCard on x0x/symphony/workers/v1 with capabilities, sandbox levels supported, available runner presets, current load, and platform info. Cards have TTL. Other daemons subscribe and maintain a worker view. Implementation: publish via x0xd's gossip pubsub HTTP surface.
acceptance: WorkerCard schema documented and versioned
Cards expire and re-publish at half-TTL
Subscriber view updates within seconds of card publication
Signed payload verified on receive

title: Live trusted-worker view drives shard slate at issue creation
description: Replace M2's static workers: list with the live worker view at issue creation. The current view epoch is recorded on the task as shard.created_view_epoch. Reshard remains an explicit operator action.
acceptance: Issue creation reads shard slate from the live worker view
shard.created_view_epoch reflects the live epoch
Static workers: block in WORKFLOW.md emits a deprecation WARN
Tests cover view churn during creation

title: Sandbox profiles in shell runner (firejail / sandbox-exec)
description: Shell runner takes a Sandbox parameter. Linux: firejail wrapper with profile per level (read-only, repo-write, no-network, full-dev, ci-only). macOS: sandbox-exec with profile. Other OS: refuse to run if profile is non-trivial. Each profile mapped to a set of allowed paths / network reach. Documented in docs/symphony/security.md.
acceptance: Five sandbox profiles implemented and tested on macOS and Linux
Profile mapping documented in docs/symphony/security.md
Refusal-to-run path tested on unsupported OS
No production unwrap/expect/panic

title: Sensitive-task gates: Pinned identity + human approval
description: Tasks labelled security-sensitive require: claimer's TrustLevel == Pinned (per ADR-0002 plus x0x trust model), and a human approval step recorded as a signed ApprovalEvent on the task. Orchestrator refuses to dispatch without an ApprovalEvent. CLI: `x0x-symphony approve <issue>` issues the signed event.
acceptance: security-sensitive tasks blocked without ApprovalEvent
ApprovalEvent is signed and verifiable
CLI subcommand x0x-symphony approve documented
Integration test covers the full gated dispatch flow

title: Partition reunion stress test on a multi-host harness
description: Two-host harness running x0x-symphonyd separated by a configurable network partition. Test sequence: create task, partition, both sides try to claim, observe sharded ownership behaviour, heal partition, verify reunion produces a correct outcome (lower-index slot wins, loser writes abandon-record).
acceptance: Two-host harness reproducible from a single shell script
Test covers create / partition / dual-claim / heal / reunion
Lower-index tiebreak verified end-to-end
Abandon record visible in proofs of the loser

title: Deprecate legacy codex: WORKFLOW.md block (warn + cutover plan)
description: WORKFLOW.md's legacy codex: block emits a WARN on load with a link to docs/symphony/operator.md describing the cutover to runner: kind=shell preset=codex. Document in CHANGELOG. Removal lands at M5 in XSY-0031.
acceptance: WARN emitted on codex: block load
Cutover documented in operator guide
CHANGELOG entry added
Removal tracked as XSY-0031

title: Remove legacy codex: WORKFLOW.md block; emit hard error on load
description: Drop support for the legacy codex: block in WORKFLOW.md. Loading a WORKFLOW.md that contains a top-level codex: block now produces a structured error referencing the runner: replacement. Update sample WORKFLOW.md files in this repo and in x0x.
acceptance: codex: block produces a structured error on load
Sample WORKFLOW.md files cleaned up in both repos
CHANGELOG breaking-change note
No silent fallthrough

title: Observability: status / workers / dashboard endpoints
description: Polish observability surfaces. CLI: `x0x-symphony status` (active claims, retry queue, last events, validation status), `x0x-symphony workers` (live worker view with capabilities). Daemon REST: GET /symphony/tasks, /symphony/tasks/:id, /symphony/workers, /symphony/proofs/:id/:ts/{stdout,stderr,manifest.json}, /symphony/events (SSE). All documented in docs/symphony/operator.md.
acceptance: All listed CLI subcommands and endpoints implemented
SSE stream documented and tested
Operator guide section: Observability
No undocumented endpoints
validation: Manual smoke against a running daemon

title: Proof-artefact reaper (retention.proofs_days)
description: Background task in x0x-symphonyd reaps proofs older than retention.proofs_days (default 30). Configurable per WORKFLOW.md. Race-safe with running orchestrator (skips dirs whose issue is currently in-progress).
acceptance: Reaper deletes proofs older than configured retention
Race-safe with active in-progress issues
retention.proofs_days documented in WORKFLOW.md schema
Tested with a synthetic clock

title: README quickstart, CHANGELOG, operator-guide polish
description: Verify the README quickstart on a clean macOS and Linux machine. Write CHANGELOG covering M0..M5. Polish docs/symphony/operator.md with a complete worked example, common pitfalls, and a troubleshooting matrix.
acceptance: Quickstart verified on a clean macOS machine
Quickstart verified on a clean Linux machine
CHANGELOG covers M0..M5 with breaking-change markers
Operator guide includes a worked example end-to-end

title: Publish to crates.io: x0x-symphony-core, all adapters, bin
description: Run `cargo publish --dry-run` clean on every member crate. Publish in dependency order. Document the publish order in docs/symphony/release-process.md. Crates.io categories and keywords set on all crates.
acceptance: cargo publish --dry-run clean on every member
All crates published in dependency order
Release process documented
Crates.io categories and keywords set

title: v0.1.0 release: tag, GitHub release, signed binaries
description: Tag v0.1.0. release.yml builds and signs binaries for the standard target matrix. GitHub release created with notes derived from CHANGELOG. Obsidian vault MOC updated to reflect v0.1.0. Final M5 gate.
acceptance: Tag v0.1.0 pushed
release.yml produces signed binaries for the full matrix
GitHub release created with CHANGELOG-derived notes
Obsidian vault MOC reflects v0.1.0
